QF.id: Evaluating Fund Performance and Fund Selection_48

question.text: Which of the following statements about CAGR is INCORRECT?

answer.text: CAGR smooths out the volatility of year-by-year returns
CAGR shows the actual return earned in each individual year
CAGR represents the average annual growth rate assuming compounding
CAGR is calculated using the beginning value, ending value, and time period

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CAGR represents the smoothed average annual growth rate, NOT the actual year-by-year return. Actual returns may vary significantly each year while CAGR remains constant. **The Trap:** Candidates may overlook the word 'INCORRECT' and pick a true statement. CAGR specifically does NOT show individual year returns — it assumes a constant growth rate. **Cross-Exam Context:** The textbook explicitly states CAGR represents the average annual growth rate, not the actual year-by-year return. This distinction is frequently tested. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When presenting CAGR to clients, clarify that actual annual returns will differ. A fund with 10% CAGR may have had years of -15% and +35% — CAGR masks this volatility.
